title: PlausiDen — about
description: An independent platform shop building software that lasts.

sections.title: An independent platform shop.
sections.lede: PlausiDen is built by a small team that thinks software should be auditable, accessible, and reproducible — by default, not as a checkbox.

sections.text: Every commit produces a deterministic artifact plus a signed audit report. The reports are stored alongside the source — anyone can verify any historical build. The discipline gate refuses to merge code that doesn't carry inline tests, inline docs, and a passing audit. We don't have a separate QA pass because the build is the QA pass.

Slow on purpose. We don't release on a calendar; we release when the discipline gate is green and the audit chain is intact. A bad release isn't a missed deadline — it's an apology to every team running the previous version. We've never shipped a CVE because we ship slowly and audit obsessively.

Revenue-funded. No venture capital. Grants where they align with the privacy-first internet (Tor Project, NLnet, NGI). Our pricing is honest: free for solo, scale by use, no per-seat tax. The platform stays independent because the funding stays independent.

items.value: Tests + docs + audit on every commit

items.hint: If the types compile, the system doesn't break.
Independently verifiable; not a trust-the-vendor claim.
No third-party fonts, no analytics, no telemetry, no CDNs.

items.value: Your content is JSON in git
items.hint: Walk away with a tar.gz; the platform produces no proprietary artifact.

sections.title: Say hello.
sections.lede: Working on something where this would help? Curious how a typed substrate would change your stack? We read every email.
